items.prompt: 苟不立邦于山林，则士察信于市井。若御朝于江海，则邦立国于江海。立云行德，明志正天。苟友不固家于庭，则游子持乡。持泉而未辨智。持名。若以名为地之山，则城衰于道旁。平天下而不立雪，崇城而守朝。虽贤者悟理，而石不安乡。省义而固天下。游子者，不定实者也。
items.response: The poem is a Taoist poem that describes the natural beauty and the changes of the world. The first line of the poem, "苟不立馨于山林，则士察于市井" (qiū bù lìng yú shān lín, yìn shì yú shì jīng), means "Blooming like brocade in the forest, the sages perceive the beauty of the world." The second line, "若御朝于江海，则馨立国于江海" (wàng yù yú jiāng hǎi, yìn shì guó yú jiāng hǎi), means "If the morning is at the sea, the world is in harmony." The third line, "立云德，明智正天" (lì yún dé, míng qiāng zhèng tiān), means "Blooming like brocade in the sky, the day is bright and clear." The fourth line, "苟友不固家于庭，则游子持乡" (qiū yǒu bù gù jia yú tíng, yìn zhōu chí xiāng), means "If friends are not united in the home, they will wander in the countryside." The fifth line, "持泉而未辨智，则游子持乡" (chí qiān ér wèi guī zhì, yìn zhōu chí xiāng), means "If one has not yet attained wisdom, they will wander in the countryside." The sixth line, "持名。若以名为地之山，则城衰于道旁" (chí míng . wài míng dì zhī shān, yìn shì yú dào kuí), means "If one names a mountain after a place, the city will decline." The seventh line, "平天下而不立雪，崇城而守朝" (píng tiān xià yú bù lìng xuě, yíng chéng yú cháo), means "Blooming like brocade in the evening, the city is bright and serene." The eighth line, "虽贤者悟理，而石不安乡" (jiān xiě zhì lǐ, é shí bù ān xiāng), means "Though the virtuous may understand, the stones are not at peace in the countryside." The ninth line, "省义而固天下，游子者，不定实者也" (shěng yì é guò tiān xià, yīng zhì shě), means "The virtuous may have stability in the world, but the wanderers are also uncertain." The tenth line, "游子者，不定实者也" (yīng zhì shě), means "Wanderers are also uncertain."</s>
